title: Tailoring Strength and Ductility of an Fe–Mn–Al–C Low-Density Duplex Steel by Controlling the Cooling Path after Hot Rolling
abstract_summary: This study investigates the influence of cooling path after hot rolling on the microstructure and tensile properties of an Fe-8.03Mn-6.10Al-0.4C low-density duplex steel. Air cooling followed by annealing produces a duplex ferrite + austenite microstructure with high ductility, while furnace cooling leads to a banded ferrite-austenite-martensite structure that causes brittle fracture. The air-cooled annealed samples exhibit a better strength-ductility match due to transformation-induced plasticity (TRIP) effect and work hardening rate recovery at later deformation stages.
research_objective: To elucidate how different cooling paths after hot rolling affect the microstructural evolution and mechanical properties, especially strength-ductility balance, in an Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density duplex steel.
main_process_variables: hot rolling temperature range 900–1100 °C, seven passes to 4 mm sheet
homogenization at 1050 °C for 2 h, reheating at 1200 °C for 2 h
cooling rate after hot rolling: air cooling (AC) vs. furnace cooling (FC)
expected_information_axis: microstructure (phase fractions, grain size, morphology, KAM/GND density)
property (tensile stress-strain curves, work hardening rate, strength-ductility match, fracture behavior)
mechanism (austenite stability, TRIP effect, martensite cracking, delamination of banded structure)

key_sections.role: Background on low-density steels, importance of austenite stability, and motivation for studying cooling path control after hot rolling.
Details of material processing, sample designation, SEM, EBSD, XRD characterization and tensile testing.

key_sections.section: Results and Discussion / Annealed Microstructure Evolution
key_sections.role: Describes hot-rolled microstructure (banded ferrite and prior austenite, martensite fraction) and its evolution during annealing for both cooling paths, explaining phase transformations and grain refinement.

key_sections.section: Results and Discussion / Effect on the Mechanical Properties
key_sections.role: Presents tensile properties, work hardening behavior, TRIP effect, fracture surfaces, and links microstructural features (e.g., martensite, austenite stability) to strength-ductility synergy.
Summarizes the key findings regarding the influence of cooling path on microstructure and resulting mechanical properties.
